observation: Fiction books have 73% more reviews on average than Non Fiction books.
insight: Fiction titles drive significantly higher engagement and social proof than Non Fiction.
business_impact: HIGH — Fiction is more likely to create viral/community-driven sales growth.
action_required: Investigate if high reviews correlate with longevity on the bestseller list.

observation: Non Fiction is 37% more expensive on average (4.84 vs 0.85).
insight: Non Fiction readers may have a higher willingness to pay for information vs entertainment.
business_impact: MEDIUM — Higher margins in Non Fiction may offset lower review counts.
action_required: Determine if price sensitivity differs by genre for potential price optimization.

observation: Top 5 authors (Jeff Kinney, Suzanne Collins, etc.) account for 10% of all bestseller entries.
insight: The bestseller list is dominated by a small group of repeat high-performers.
business_impact: HIGH — High barriers to entry for new authors in the bestseller category.
action_required: Monitor the share of "new" vs "legacy" authors over time to identify market shifts.
